etymology.description: Phonetic 焚 (fén, 'burn')/ 棼 (fén, 'luxuriant') + semantic 彡 (shān, 'ornament') -> refined, literary
etymology.interpretation: The character combines the phonetic element 焚(焚) (fén, 'burn') or 棼(棼) (fén, 'luxuriant') to suggest sound and the semantic 彡(彡) (shān, 'ornament') which is an indicator of decoration or embellishment. Together, they create the idea of someone or something being refined or literary.

examples.chinese: 他为人温文尔雅，是个斌斌有礼的人(他為人溫文爾雅，是個斌斌有禮的人)。
examples.pinyin: Tā wéirén wēnwén'ěryǎ, shì gè bīnbīn yǒulǐ de rén.
examples.english: He is gentle and refined, a polite and courteous person.

examples.chinese: 他们的婚礼非常隆重，场面既斌且雅(他們的婚禮非常隆重，場面既斌且雅)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen de hūnlǐ fēicháng lóngzhòng, chǎngmiàn jì bīn qiě yǎ.
examples.english: Their wedding was very grand, both refined and elegant.

examples.chinese: 在新书发表会上，他的讲话文质斌斌(在新書發表會上，他的講話文質斌斌)。
examples.pinyin: Zài xīnshū fābiǎo huìshàng, tā de jiǎnghuà wén zhì bīn bīn.
examples.english: At the book launch, his speech was refined and cultured.
